questions.question: In the following question, select the odd letters from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: Except option (d) all follow the same logic as follows: BGDI B+5= G B+2= D D+5= I OTQV O+5= T O+2= Q Q+5= V INKP I+5= N I+2= K K+5= P

questions.question: Two positions of the cube are shown below. What will come opposite to the face containing "1"? ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3376/image019.jpg)
questions.explanation: After combining both positions of the cube, we will get: - ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3376/image020.png) So, 2 will come opposite to the face containing 1.

questions.question: In the following question, select the odd-one out from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: Except option (c), all follow the same logic as follows: Option (a): 3³, (3-1)³, (3-2)³ Option (b): 9³, (9-1)³, (9-2)³ Option (d): 13³, (13-1)³, (13-2)³

questions.question: In the following question, correct the equation by interchanging two signs. 125 - 12 X 2 ÷ 24 + 4 = -15
questions.explanation: On interchanging X and ÷ signs we get, LHS = 125 - 12 ÷ 2 X 24 + 4 = 125 - 6 X 24 + 4 = 125 - 144 + 4 = -15

questions.question: There are six friends in a group. K is heavier than at least four persons. L is the lightest one. Q is heavier than S, who is not lighter than M. P is not heavier than S. M is heavier than P. Who is the third heaviest person?
questions.explanation: → M is heavier than P. → P is not heavier than S. We get, M > S > P Or S > M > P Now, Q is heavier than S And S is not lighter than M. So, we get, S > M > P is only valid. We have, Q > S > M > P → L is the lightest one. So, Q > S > M > P > L And, K is heavier than at least four persons. So, Cases possible are: K > Q > S > M > P > L Or Q > K > S > M > P > L But, the third heaviest remains the same in both cases. Thus, S is the third heaviest person.

questions.question: In the questions below there are four statements and four conclusions in the options. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts and then decide which of the following given conclusions logically follow(s) from the four statements disregarding commonly known facts: Statements: Some Birds are Girls Some Boys are Humans All Birds are Creatures Many Birds are Boys Conclusions: I: Some Boys are Girls II: Some Creatures are Boys III: Some Humans are Birds IV: No Boys are Girls
options: II and Either I or IV
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/35960/image007.png)

questions.question: Statement: People mostly prefer high speed net connections. Conclusions: I) High speed net connections are very cheap. II) They don't want to wait for long time waiting for their pages to load.
options: Neither Conclusion I nor II follows.
questions.explanation: Conclusion I doesn't logically follow the statement. But people who prefer high net speed prefer so because they don't like to wait and waste their time.

questions.question: Pointing to a photograph, Kunal said "That person's brother is the brother-in-law of my father's only daughter." How is the person in the photograph related to Kunal?
questions.explanation: The person in the photograph is the husband of Kunal's only sister. So, that person must be the brother-in-law of Kunal. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/51444/image001.jpg)

questions.question: From the given alternative words, select the word which cannot be formed using the letters of the given word 'passionate'?
questions.explanation: The word SENATE cannot be formed as there are two E's present in it while the given word has only one.

questions.question: Choose a figure which would most closely resemble the unfolded form of figures after folding and cutting. Problem figure ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/sscimg/6700/image014.png) Answer figures ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/sscimg/6700/image015.png)
questions.explanation: After first unfold, the right half will have two circles and a triangle. After final unfold, there will be four circles in the centre and two triangles near the two vertices.

questions.question: Arrange the following words as per the order in dictionary. 1. Sacrilege 2. Sacred 3. Sacramento 4. Sacrificial 5. Sacrifice
questions.explanation: Sacramento Sacred Sacrifice Sacrificial Sacrilege This is the correct form as per the order in dictionary.

questions.question: Which set of letters when sequentially placed at the gaps in the given letter series shall complete It? j j _ _ j _ k _ j _ k i
questions.explanation: The correct sequence is jjkijjkijjki

questions.question: A word is represented by only one set of number is given in any one of the alternatives. The sets of number given in the alternatives are represented by two classes of alphabets as in two matrices given below. The column and rows of matrix I are numbered from 0 to 4 and that of matrix II are numbered from 5 to 9.A letter from these matrices can be represented first by its row and next by its column, e.g; S can be represented by 41,14,etc and O can be represented by 31,23,etc,similarly,you have to identify the set for the word SORT. MATRIX-I 01234 0SMNOL 1NLOMS 2LSNOM 3NOLSM 4LSOMN MATRIX-Ii 56789 5QRUTV 6URTVQ 7TVRUQ 8URVQT 9RVTUQ
questions.explanation: By verifying using 1ˢᵗ option 41 42 97 77 S O T R By 2ⁿᵈ option 33 12 66 89 S O R T.

questions.question: Ram is facing north. Which sequence of the following turns will lead him to face west?
questions.explanation: North + Left, Left, Left = East North + Right, Left, Right = East North + Right, Right, Left = East North + Left, Right, Left = West

questions.question: Which answer figure completes the pattern in the question figure? ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1791/image038.png) A. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1791/image039.png) B. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1791/image040.png) C. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1791/image041.png) D. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1791/image042.png)

questions.question: In a certain code 'SALTED' is coded as 'MBTWVG' then how will 'WANDER' be coded as?
questions.explanation: The word is divided into two parts. In the first part, the code is obtained by adding 1 to the respective alphabets and in the second part the opposite position value alphabets are replaced by forward position value alphabets. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1777/image012.jpg)

questions.question: If a mirror is placed on the given line, then which of the answer figure is right image of the given figure? ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1791/image023.png) A. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1791/image024.png) B. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1791/image025.png) C. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1791/image026.png) D. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1791/image027.png)
questions.explanation: In options 1 and 2, position of circle is wrong. In option 3, position of circle and inclination of all the lines inside the square are wrong. Option 4 is the exact mirror image of the question figure and hence the correct choice.
